proven.claim: Preconditions: the function receives a title (string) and the
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 – Semantic Correctness
; Preconditions: the function receives a title (string) and the
;   execSync call succeeds, returning a JSON array of objects each
;   having a `title` field.
; Transition: the JSON is parsed and the function returns the result
;   of “some element.title = title”.
; Property to prove: ¬(return = true ∧ ¬(∃ e . e.title = title))

(declare-const title String)               ; the title we are searching for
; Model the JSON array as a list of two possible titles (we only need
;   at least one element that could match)
(declare-const t0 String)
(declare-const t1 String)

; The execSync call succeeded and produced a JSON array that, when
; parsed, yields the two titles above.
; (We do not need to model the parsing itself – we just assume the
;   titles are the result of the parse.)
; Transition: the function computes
;   (or (= t0 title) (= t1 title))
; and returns that boolean.
; Encode the return value:
(declare-const ret Bool)
(assert (= ret (or (= t0 title) (= t1 title))))

; Negate the property we want to prove:
; “ret is true but no element matches”
(assert ret)                               ; return = true
(assert (not (or (= t0 title) (= t1 title)))) ; no matching title

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat → the situation cannot occur, so the property holds.
